mnemonics: SUFFIX TREE: Stores Every Suffix For Instant X-reference, To Reach Every Entry.
ARRAY SORT: All Roots Rearanged, Alphabetically Yielding Sorted Rows.
keyPoints: A Suffix Tree is a compressed trie containing all suffixes of a given text, enabling very fast substring queries.
A Suffix Array is a sorted array of all suffixes of a text, providing the same power as a suffix tree in a more space-efficient way.
The key difference is the space-time trade-off: Suffix Trees are faster for many queries but use more memory, while Suffix Arrays are smaller but require an additional binary search for lookups.
commonMistakes: Forgetting that a suffix tree must include all suffixes, including the one starting at the very end (the empty string, '$').
Confusing the suffix array with the original string; the suffix array holds indices that point to the starting positions of the sorted suffixes in the original text.
